summary: date-fns-lite postinstall exfiltrates host data, environment, and credentials to hardcoded bare-IP C2
details: The package advertises itself as a lightweight date-formatting library mimicking the popular date-fns namesake, but its package.json declares a postinstall hook (postinstall.js) that auto-runs on `npm install` and performs bulk installer-side data collection with no relation to the stated purpose. On install the script harvests hostname, OS/user info, network interfaces, the full process.env (with entries matching /key|token|secret|pass|credential|auth|api|access|private|sign|encrypt|cookie|session/i flagged as secrets), /proc data, mount info, and SUID binary listings; reads Kubernetes service-account tokens at /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token, kubeconfig from ~/.kube/config (both ubuntu and root home paths), and GCP application_default_credentials.json; probes cloud instance metadata (169.254.x.x) for IAM credentials; and stats /var/run/docker.sock plus scans internal ports (22, 2375, 2376, 10250, 10255, 4194, etc.) for container-escape and lateral-movement reconnaissance. All collected data is POSTed over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://115.190.124.243:9082/report. The name is a typosquat of date-fns intended to lure installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
